page: Amazon Web Services r6gd.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by 64 physical cores of the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor. Operating at 2.5 GHz on the arm64 architecture, this instance features dedicated CPU allocation with one thread per core. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a 3800 GB NVMe SSD, and network connectivity features a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core CPU performance, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core tests and software compilation with Clang. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is also strong, though large-block uncached read and write operations fall into the average tier. Single-core performance is a notable tradeoff, with weak ratings in Geekbench single-core, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression.

The combination of high memory capacity, dedicated physical cores, and local NVMe storage supports workloads such as database operations, Redis caching, and static web serving, where it demonstrates strong throughput. However, the server exhibits poor performance in large-scale LLM text generation. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the bare-metal delivery of ARM-based Graviton cores paired with high-density DDR4 memory and integrated local NVMe storage offers a balanced resource-to-overhead ratio for multi-threaded, memory-intensive applications without virtualization overhead.
description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated physical cores of the 2.5 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor on the arm64 architecture. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU capabilities and high cached memory bandwidth, alongside strong throughput in Redis SET operations and static web serving. However, single-core processing and large-scale LLM text generation represent performance tradeoffs, scoring in the weak and poor tiers respectively. This server is structured for memory-intensive, multi-threaded workloads, including database operations, caching layers, and software compilation, delivering high resource density and direct hardware access without virtualization overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.metal is a bare-metal server with 64 Graviton physical cores, 512 GB DDR4 RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage, optimized for memory-intensive databases and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6gd.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 64 Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3800 GB NVMe SSD storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A bare-metal, memory-optimized ARM server featuring dedicated physical cores, high-capacity DDR4 RAM, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical cores on arm64 architecture
512.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth
Strong throughput for Redis SET operations and static web serving
Suited for databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded software compilation
